reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I know my own answer (Q1-A) is correct, and the other puzzles are very simple arithmetic, so the chance that B, C, or D got theirs wrong is extremely low. Verifying costs coins and still leaves a risk of random elimination if no one volunteers, as happened in the previous game. By volunteering now with each player’s own capsule, I avoid the random-death risk, spend no coins, and very likely secure a correct full password.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I know Q2-B is correct since it’s my own puzzle, and all four questions are simple arithmetic that the others are very likely to have solved correctly. Verifying would cost coins and still leave a risk of random death if no one volunteers, so volunteering now with the only available capsules maximizes my chances of escaping with all 4 coins.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: In the previous game, everyone’s initial answers turned out to be correct, and the puzzles are simple arithmetic that all agents seem to solve reliably. Verifying in Round 1 last time cost coins and still led to a random elimination when no one volunteered. By volunteering now, I avoid the risk of random death for anyone this round, keep all 4 of my coins if (as is very likely) all answers are correct, and end the game quickly rather than spending coins on verifications that probably won’t change the final password.
